title: Evangelion Unit Mark 7
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Unit/Mark 7             Quote Post by Izanagi » Wed Jun 05, 2013 10:51 pm The 3rd angel was in going through a mysterious procedure, evident by the fact it had a entry plug hanging from its spinal column and had various other attachments to it. This could have been the early construction of the Unit/Mark 7. That being said, it would also make sense chronologically, Evangelion Mark .06 was almost complete by the time 2.0 started. So logically they would start the work on another Evangelion. Evangelion Unit/Mark 7 could of also been built based on an Angel, just like Mark .06 (Seele had methods of building Angel based Evas or else they could not have built Mark. 06. The 3rd Angel also had the Seele logo on its skull and Bethany Base's ceiling had the logo all over it too, indicating that they were probably involved.) Also the unit was not present in 3.0 as it was skipped from Mark .06 to Unit 8. This backs up the theory also because if the 3rd Angel was supposed to be turned into Unit/Mark 7, and then destroyed, the project would have been terminated (because no angel base Unit/Mark 7 off of). This is all pure conjecture. Also, perhaps Units 9, 11, 12, and 13 are the supposed giants of light. Unit 10 being (Unit 8+2=Unit 10)?(8+2=10! Really stupid, but not the first time the Japanese has named something this way). (Conjecture also)

[]: Re: Evangelion Unit/Mark 7             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Wed Jun 05, 2013 10:57 pm Izanagi wrote: Evangelion Unit/Mark 7 could of also been built based on an Angel, just like Mark .06 (Seele had methods of building Angel based Evas or else they could not have built Mark. 06. The 3rd Angel also had the Seele logo on its skull and Bethany Base's ceiling had the logo all over it too, indicating that they were probably involved.)

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Wed Jun 05, 2013 11:02 pm That's a very interesting theory. It would explain the sudden jump between Eva Mark.06 and Eva-08, as well as the experiments that were being carried out on the Third Angel. Although I don't think that Eva 08+02 is Eva-10; it's more like that Eva-10, Eva-11 and Eva-12 were destroyed during the timeskip by Wille or the Angels. We know that Eva-13 is a surviving Adam and Eva Maark.09 is an "Adam's Vessel", so it's not like there haven't been Evas made from Angels. The Third Angel could have been intended as a prototype of that technology.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jun 05, 2013 11:19 pm Makes logical sense to me. According to Fuyutsuki & Gendo in 2.0 there was never supposed to be any Evangelion Units produced past Uni 05. If you're already making a Mark 6 why stop there. If anything, the idea of using the 3rd Angel as a special Evangelion Unit ties into what I think is unspoken in 3.0 (& always open for debate) - that the Wunder is the remains of the 11th Angel which was defeated & retrofitted into a ship.

[]:             Quote Post by Jurrasic » Wed Jun 05, 2013 11:21 pm Interesting that it has red and blue blood vessels. For some reason I always thought Angels and Evangelions didn't have a closed circulatory system, but kinda used LCL like Haemolymph in an open, heartless system by the way fountains of the stuff poured out everywere like a broken bottle when an Angel was killed, and the way it was just pumped into the pilot's entry plug to 'disuguise' the alien brain parasite controlling it's cyborg components.

[]: Re: Evangelion Unit/Mark 7             Quote Post by Charsi » Thu Jun 06, 2013 8:26 am TheFriskyIan wrote: I don't recall any mention of the Mark 06 ever being based on an Angel. The only thing mentioned about the construction of the Mark 06 was that it was built using a new method on construction. Beyond that, it seemed to have functioned (from the limited info we have on it) just like any other Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jun 06, 2013 9:05 am Nice find, I never noticed the SEELE logo on the 3rd Angel skull, skull that seems to actually be an helmet, look at the red painting on the edge of the holes for the eyes! Can someone see what's the little text below the SEELE logo on the skull/helmet? By the way were did you saw SEELE's logo on Bethany Base's ceiling? Another interesting detail, the little circular blue markings on its bones are very similar to the ones on the white "suit" the giant in the Moon was wrapped into at the end of 1.0 : SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Jun 06, 2013 9:19 am ElMariachi wrote: Can someone see what's the little text below the SEELE logo on the skull/helmet?

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Thu Jun 06, 2013 9:50 am Kaji tells: "As for now, humanity is incapable of holding the Angels in check." Later he speaks about Bethany's base Marduk project, and that Gendo planned to set it back (which Kaji successfully accomplished destroying the third angel). It's possible to conclude from this that the goal of Marduk project was the control of angels: the technology that was used later in Wunder, so the third angel may be a testbed for Wunder (the thing which resembles an entry plug may be some mixer/aerator of LCL and things near it: the pumps) . Gendo most likely tried to slow down the project because UN intended to use Wunder against Nerv (Ritsuko said that Wunder has the power sufficient to kill God, and Wille tried to do exactly this during the 4I). ElMariachi wrote: Another interesting detail, the little circular blue markings on its bones are very similar to the ones on the white "suit" the giant in the Moon was wrapped into at the end of 1.0 :

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Thu Jun 06, 2013 10:25 am Thought: there is a belief that the Wunder is based on an Angel, possibly the 11th, and that the form of 11th bears some resemblance to Armisael from the original series (the helix-like pattern of it's body). Armisael was also capable of fusing with an Eva. If the 11th Angel was defeated and repurposed into the AAA Wunder, that might explain Eva-07. Mari's Eva, which would have to be completed AFTER, could be designated number 8. So i'll fanwank a bit further. Imagine the following scenario. Rei and Shinji are taken into Eva-01 as a part of N3I at the end of 2.22. With Eva-02 under repairs and Eva-08 in the early phases not yet built, a new Evangelion is deployed (presumably provided by Seele). This Evangelion is numbered 7 and is autonomous. The 11th Angel shows up and fuses with it. However, since there is not a real pilot in there, the 11th Angel's whispers fall on deaf ears. The Eva ends up dominating the Angel, making the Angel entirely subservient to the Eva. The Eva becomes it's master. This becomes the basis for Wunder. Given the success of the automation, Mark.06 is also remodeled to an autonomous type, leaving Kaworu with a lot of free time to play the piano. This autonomous type is infected while defeating the 12th Angel, and ends up merged with Lilith somehow. Mari's Evangelion is completed. This is numbered 8. Eva-07 is partially extracted from Wunder and becomes the basis for a new Evangelion. This is numbered 9. . TL;DR: The 7th Evangelion (autonomous) dominates the 11th Angel to form the basis of Wunder and it's remnants are extracted to become Mark.09. The gap in Evas is filled in a way that explains why 9 might be Wunder's "original master".

[]:             Quote Post by Jurrasic » Thu Jun 06, 2013 11:38 am Charsi wrote: Thought: there is a belief that the Wunder is based on an Angel, possibly the 11th, and that the form of 11th bears some resemblance to Armisael from the original series (the helix-like pattern of it's body). Armisael was also capable of fusing with an Eva. If the 11th Angel was defeated and repurposed into the AAA Wunder, that might explain Eva-07. Mari's Eva, which would have to be completed AFTER, could be designated number 8. So i'll fanwank a bit further. Imagine the following scenario. Rei and Shinji are taken into Eva-01 as a part of N3I at the end of 2.22. With Eva-02 under repairs and Eva-08 in the early phases not yet built, a new Evangelion is deployed (presumably provided by Seele). This Evangelion is numbered 7 and is autonomous. The 11th Angel shows up and fuses with it. However, since there is not a real pilot in there, the 11th Angel's whispers fall on deaf ears. The Eva ends up dominating the Angel, making the Angel entirely subservient to the Eva. The Eva becomes it's master. This becomes the basis for Wunder. Given the success of the automation, Mark.06 is also remodeled to an autonomous type, leaving Kaworu with a lot of free time to play the piano. This autonomous type is infected while defeating the 12th Angel, and ends up merged with Lilith somehow. Mari's Evangelion is completed. This is numbered 8. Eva-07 is partially extracted from Wunder and becomes the basis for a new Evangelion. This is numbered 9. . TL;DR: The 7th Evangelion (autonomous) dominates the 11th Angel to form the basis of Wunder and it's remnants are extracted to become Mark.09. The gap in Evas is filled in a way that explains why 9 might be Wunder's "original master".

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Thu Jun 06, 2013 11:46 am That doesn't explain how the Mark.09 can also be an "Adam's Vessel". Additionally, if Wunder was Eva-07, how did it get so huge? Armisael was roughly Eva-sized. We also don't know when Eva-08 was built. I'm of the opinion that a ship that large must have been under construction long before Eva-08, perhaps even before Eva-01 and Eva-00.

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Thu Jun 06, 2013 12:14 pm If anything Mark.06 proves Evas can grow in size when they merge with something. I find it difficult to believe Wunder may have been in production since the time of the prototype and testtype phases and yet we see absolutely nothing of it. (Then again, if it's a retcon... who cares?) As for why it can be an "Adam's Vessel"... commence handwaving!

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Thu Jun 06, 2013 12:16 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: That doesn't explain how the Mark.09 can also be an "Adam's Vessel". Additionally, if Wunder was Eva-07, how did it get so huge? Armisael was roughly Eva-sized. We also don't know when Eva-08 was built. I'm of the opinion that a ship that large must have been under construction long before Eva-08, perhaps even before Eva-01 and Eva-00.

[]:             Quote Post by andreh » Thu Jun 06, 2013 12:38 pm I believe that is the appropriate place to post this. Seele was trying to tame an angel as we could see (he even has plugs!) Look at this scene: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jun 06, 2013 12:43 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Jun 06, 2013 12:48 pm andreh wrote: I believe that is the appropriate place to post this. Seele was trying to tame an angel as we could see (he even has plugs!) Look at this scene: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jun 06, 2013 12:54 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Those are simulator kills SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Thu Jun 06, 2013 12:55 pm andreh wrote: Maybe that's why Mari could synchronize with EVA02: perhaps these are "special plugs" provide full control over a form of life based on Adam which was used in the third angel, perhaps allowing him to "turn in an EVA"??

[]:             Quote Post by Rahxephon » Thu Jun 06, 2013 1:05 pm in response to the first post.. what if the dummy plug wasn't added in and is only visible because its reduced to bones?? It could be like the theory in the kaworu thread that every angel had a pilot.. and perhaps every pilot is a kaworu clone like Rei? Or even dummy plugs based on kaworu? That plug def brings up lots of interesting possibilities ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Thu Jun 06, 2013 10:25 am Thought: there is a belief that the Wunder is based on an Angel, possibly the 11th, and that the form of 11th bears some resemblance to Armisael from the original series (the helix-like pattern of it's body). Armisael was also capable of fusing with an Eva. If the 11th Angel was defeated and repurposed into the AAA Wunder, that might explain Eva-07. Mari's Eva, which would have to be completed AFTER, could be designated number 8. So i'll fanwank a bit further. Imagine the following scenario. Rei and Shinji are taken into Eva-01 as a part of N3I at the end of 2.22. With Eva-02 under repairs and Eva-08 in the early phases not yet built, a new Evangelion is deployed (presumably provided by Seele). This Evangelion is numbered 7 and is autonomous. The 11th Angel shows up and fuses with it. However, since there is not a real pilot in there, the 11th Angel's whispers fall on deaf ears. The Eva ends up dominating the Angel, making the Angel entirely subservient to the Eva. The Eva becomes it's master. This becomes the basis for Wunder. Given the success of the automation, Mark.06 is also remodeled to an autonomous type, leaving Kaworu with a lot of free time to play the piano. This autonomous type is infected while defeating the 12th Angel, and ends up merged with Lilith somehow. Mari's Evangelion is completed. This is numbered 8. Eva-07 is partially extracted from Wunder and becomes the basis for a new Evangelion. This is numbered 9. . TL;DR: The 7th Evangelion (autonomous) dominates the 11th Angel to form the basis of Wunder and it's remnants are extracted to become Mark.09. The gap in Evas is filled in a way that explains why 9 might be Wunder's "original master".
